title: fragaria nilgerrensis subsp. hayatae
intro: Fragaria nilgerrensis subsp. hayatae, also known as the Japanese strawberry or Hayata's strawberry, is a subspecies of the Fragaria nilgerrensis species, which is a type of strawberry native to East Asia, particularly in China, Japan, and Southeast Asia. This plant is used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Fragaria nilgerrensis subsp. hayatae include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its extracts are also used in modern pharmacology for the production of herbal supplements and medications.
